title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Here’s what happened in crypto today
briefing: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Kaspersky identifies malware framework targeting crypto investors
summary: Kaspersky identifies malware framework targeting crypto investors
briefing: Cybersecurity company Kaspersky said a newly identified malware framework is targeting cryptocurrency investors through social engineering tactics and trojanized GitHub apps.

title: French gambling regulator orders ISPs to block Polymarket
summary: French gambling regulator orders ISPs to block Polymarket
briefing: France’s gambling authority has ordered the country’s internet service providers to geoblock Polymarket, citing illegal gambling and market manipulation concerns.

title: FTX to distribute $900M to creditors in fifth payment round
summary: FTX to distribute $900M to creditors in fifth payment round
briefing: The FTX Recovery Trust and company have distributed about $10 billion since the exchange filed for bankruptcy in November 2022, leaving users cut off from their funds.

title: Galaxy lands 15-year Texas Tech stadium naming rights deal
summary: Galaxy lands 15-year Texas Tech stadium naming rights deal
briefing: Galaxy Digital will rename Texas Tech’s football stadium under a 15-year agreement, expanding its West Texas presence as the state attracts growing crypto investment.

title: Consensys unknowingly outsourced developer work to North Korean
summary: Consensys unknowingly outsourced developer work to North Korean
briefing: Through an introduction with a “reputable third-party service provider,“ the company took on a developer who, as part of an investigation, was revealed to be tied to North Korea.

title: Crypto Biz: When dollars disappear, stablecoins step in
summary: Crypto Biz: When dollars disappear, stablecoins step in
briefing: Bolivia moves to recognize USDT amid a dollar shortage, while Bitcoin miners’ AI ambitions face fresh investor scrutiny.

title: OKX Europe lets users convert USDT to MiCA-compliant USDC
summary: OKX Europe lets users convert USDT to MiCA-compliant USDC
briefing: The feature offers European customers a voluntary path away from Tether’s USDT as MiCA rules reshape the region’s stablecoin market.

title: ECB Warns Stablecoins May Drain Bank Deposits—Here's What That Means
summary: ECB Warns Stablecoins May Drain Bank Deposits—Here's What That Means
briefing: ECB board member Piero Cipollone laid out the three-layer threat banks face from digital payments, and pitched the digital euro as the only structural answer.

title: Cardano Pumps as Network Moves to Further Decentralize Development
summary: Cardano Pumps as Network Moves to Further Decentralize Development
briefing: Input Output is handing its core infrastructure to outside teams as ADA gets a lift from an imminent protocol upgrade.

title: Kimi K3 Just Triggered DeepSeek Flashbacks for the Stock Market
summary: Kimi K3 Just Triggered DeepSeek Flashbacks for the Stock Market
briefing: Moonshot AI's 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model sent chip stocks tumbling and gave Wall Street a Friday it would rather forget.

title: China’s Kimi K3 Is Out—And Beats Claude Fable and GPT 5.6 Sol on Key Benchmarks
summary: China’s Kimi K3 Is Out—And Beats Claude Fable and GPT 5.6 Sol on Key Benchmarks
briefing: Moonshot AI's 2.8-trillion-parameter model tops Fable 5 on a creative writing benchmark and leads Arena AI's frontend code leaderboard—at Claude Sonnet pricing.

title: Galaxy Puts Its Name on Texas Tech's Stadium in a 15-Year, Crypto-Native Deal
summary: Galaxy Puts Its Name on Texas Tech's Stadium in a 15-Year, Crypto-Native Deal
briefing: The Nasdaq-listed digital-asset firm will rebrand the Red Raiders' home as "Galaxy Stadium." The bigger bet is on the cheap power and open land of West Texas.

title: Three Men Jailed for Posing as Police in $5.3M UK Crypto Fraud
summary: Three Men Jailed for Posing as Police in $5.3M UK Crypto Fraud
briefing: The gang built fake police websites to fool victims, then spent the stolen crypto on Rolexes and luxury holidays, the Met said.

title: Morning Minute: Citadel Securities Invests $400M in Crypto.com
summary: Morning Minute: Citadel Securities Invests $400M in Crypto.com
briefing: Wall Street and crypto's convergence snapped into focus, while crypto majors gave back most of the week's gains as broader markets sold off.

title: Bitcoin Tests $63K as Long-Term Holders Keep Selling at a Loss
summary: Bitcoin Tests $63K as Long-Term Holders Keep Selling at a Loss
briefing: Some two-thirds of the coins moving onto exchanges are long-term holders taking losses, as a macro risk-off drags on Bitcoin’s price.
